_meta.source: orasleep.ch scraped data + kick-off meeting notes
_meta.philosophy: MAXIMALE DIVERSITY — Andromeda Strategy. These are starting points and inspiration, NOT rigid rules. Performance > Brand consistency. Every creative should look different.

typography.diversity_note: Jost is the website font but creatives can use ANY font that fits the concept. 80+ fonts are in active use across campaigns. Mix serif, sans-serif, display, handwritten — whatever performs. No font is mandatory.

colors.diversity_note: These are the website colors, not creative constraints. Use them as a starting point or ignore them entirely. Bold neons, pastels, gradients, black-and-white, warm earth tones — whatever drives performance. The best-performing ad ('93 Prozent') used simple high-contrast text, not brand colors.

visual_style.website_direction: Clean, minimalist, Swiss design. High contrast. White space.
visual_style.diversity_note: The website is minimalist but creatives are NOT limited to this style. Experiment freely: bold typography, UGC-style, editorial, data-visualization, meme formats, testimonial cards, comparison layouts, product close-ups, abstract, collage — anything goes. Top performers are often the SIMPLEST creatives. No logo required. No headline required. No fixed element order.

tone_of_voice.language: German (Swiss market), occasional Swiss German in UGC/reviews
spectrum: Formal/expert: Scientific claims, test results, comparisons
Direct/urgent: Pain points, poor sleep consequences
tone_of_voice.diversity_note: Vary tone dramatically between creatives. One ad can be a clinical data point, the next a raw emotional testimonial, the next a provocative question. Match tone to audience segment and placement.

trust_signals.usage_note: Use trust signals flexibly — sometimes a single data point is more powerful than showing everything. '143 Fünf-Sterne-Bewertungen' can hit harder than a generic badge. Mix and match. Sometimes use none at all.

ad_formats.note: These are canvas sizes, not layout constraints. Fill them however the creative demands.

diversity_rules.philosophy: Andromeda Strategy — cast a wide net with maximum creative variety to find what resonates with different audience segments.
principles: Every creative should look visually distinct from the last
No fixed templates — structure follows concept
Simple often beats complex (the '93 Prozent' ad principle)
Performance data overrides any brand rule
Test wildly different approaches: data-driven, emotional, UGC, editorial, minimal, maximal
Different audience segments need different visual languages
If something looks too 'same-y' as previous creatives, change it
anti_patterns: DO NOT create a fixed template system
DO NOT require logo on every creative
DO NOT require headline on every creative
DO NOT stick to one color palette
DO NOT use the same layout twice in a row
DO NOT prioritize brand consistency over performance
